rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE syntax, with type scopes for assumptions/guarantees and impl scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should relate visible outputs to visible inputs without relying on implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers and typed ports; avoid AADL property names as variables.
When defining ranges, use helper declarations like eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in annex agree only when concrete bounds or named constants are provided.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign keywords with proper syntax inside annex agree blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for assignments and bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
